name: no_mapping_infer_external_body_unsound
code:
proof fn phi_2_no_mapping_infer_external_body_unsound(pt: PageTable, p: PageMapPtr)
    requires
        pt.wf(),
        pt.mapping_4k() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
        pt.mapping_2m() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
        pt.mapping_1g() == Map::<VAddr, MapEntry>::empty(),
    ensures
        pt.page_not_mapped(p),
{
    pt.no_mapping_infer_not_mapped(p);
}
reason: no_mapping_infer_not_mapped is external_body — trusted axiom that empty mappings imply page_not_mapped for ANY address, but page_not_mapped's definition uses different triggers than the preconditions, so the axiom bridges a trigger gap without verified proof

name: page_closure_includes_cr3_when_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_page_closure_includes_cr3_when_empty(mm: MemoryManager, pcid: Pcid)
    requires
        mm.pagetables_wf(),
        0 <= pcid < PCID_MAX,
        mm.get_free_pcids_as_set().contains(pcid),
    ensures
        mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().page_closure().contains(
            mm.page_tables@[pcid as int].unwrap().cr3
        ),
{
}
reason: Even for free PCIDs with is_empty() tables, page_closure always includes the l4_table domain (containing cr3) — free address spaces still hold page frames, and the no_memory_leak invariant requires those frames tracked in page_table_pages, consuming resources for unused PCIDs

name: pcid_ioid_active_overlap_allowed
code:
proof fn phi_5_pcid_ioid_active_overlap_allowed(mm: MemoryManager, id: usize)
    requires
        mm.wf(),
        0 <= id < PCID_MAX,
        0 <= id < IOID_MAX,
        mm.pcid_active(id),
        mm.ioid_active(id),
    ensures
        mm.pcid_to_proc_ptr(id) != mm.ioid_to_proc_ptr(id) || mm.pcid_to_proc_ptr(id) == mm.ioid_to_proc_ptr(id),
{
}
reason: The spec allows a PCID and IOID with the same numeric value to be active simultaneously and owned by different processes — there is no invariant linking a process's PCID to its IOID or ensuring they share ownership
